name: UNCF General Scholarship
description: Open to full-time students at UNCF member institutions demonstrating financial need. One of the most accessible and widely available scholarships for HBCU students.

name: TMCF McDonald's Black and Positively Golden Scholarship
description: 60 scholarships of $15,000 each for full-time undergraduate students at HBCUs or Predominantly Black Institutions. Requires a 100-word statement on your values.

name: KR Scholars HBCU Scholarship
description: Annual scholarship for full-time sophomore, junior, and senior HBCU students of any major who demonstrate financial need. Open to all Hampton University students.

name: ETS Presidential Scholarship for HBCU Students
description: Available to full-time junior and senior students at HBCUs. Recipients also receive mentoring, professional development, and networking opportunities with ETS leaders.

name: TMCF Emergency Assistance Scholarship
description: Emergency funding for sophomores, juniors, and seniors at TMCF partner schools experiencing exceptional financial need with an unpaid school balance.

name: HBCU Connect Student Scholarship
description: Multiple $1,000 scholarships for students currently enrolled at any HBCU. Funds can be applied to tuition or books. Simple application with a brief essay.

name: Ron Brown Scholar Program
description: Full four-year scholarship for intellectually gifted African Americans committed to community service and leadership. One of the most prestigious awards for Black students.

name: Blacks at Microsoft Scholarship
description: Supports African American undergraduate and graduate students pursuing STEM degrees with financial assistance, mentorship, and networking at Microsoft.

name: Splunk HBCU Academic Scholarship
description: 15 scholarships of $10,000 each for HBCU students pursuing technology-related degrees. Recipients also receive Splunk mentorship and an opportunity to interview for internships.

name: Google Generation Scholarship
description: Awarded to students from underrepresented groups studying computer science. Recipients are invited to attend Google's annual Scholar Retreat in Mountain View.

name: AFCEA STEM Scholarship
description: Awarded to students pursuing STEM degrees who are enrolled as full-time sophomores or juniors. Special consideration given to underrepresented minorities.

name: NACME Scholarship
organization: National Action Council for Minorities in Engineering
description: Supports minority students enrolled in engineering or computer science programs. NACME partners with over 40 universities to distribute these awards.

name: Society of Women Engineers Scholarship
description: Multiple scholarship programs supporting women in engineering and technology fields. Underrepresented minority women are especially encouraged to apply.

name: AABE National Scholarship
organization: American Association of Blacks in Energy
description: Supports minority students pursuing energy-related careers in engineering, technology, and science fields.

name: NOAA Undergraduate Scholarship
description: For STEM sophomores at Minority Serving Institutions. Includes two paid summer internships at NOAA facilities, covering travel, conference, and living allowances.

name: AICPA Minority Accounting Scholarship
description: Promotes greater ethnic and racial diversity in the CPA profession. For underrepresented minority students pursuing accounting or finance degrees.

name: Government Finance Officers Association Scholarship
description: Supports students pursuing careers in state and local government finance. Preference given to GFOA members and students with demonstrated interest in public finance.

name: National Black MBA Association Scholarship
description: Supports Black students pursuing undergraduate and MBA degrees in business-related fields. Recipients gain access to NBMBAA's national network and career development events.

name: Charles River Associates All-Access Scholarship
description: Diversity pipeline program awarding $5,000 to 12 students pursuing economics, business, or STEM. Junior-year students at accredited colleges/universities are eligible.

name: Procter & Gamble Scholarship
description: Open to minority students across a wide range of majors including accounting, finance, marketing, HR, and computer information systems. Encourages diversity in corporate America.

name: Mission North Communications Scholarship
description: For female students pursuing careers in communications. Supports diversity in PR, media, and communications industries with financial assistance and mentorship.

name: Kay Longcope Journalism Scholarship
description: For LGBTQ undergraduate and graduate students of color pursuing careers in journalism, media, policy, or communications. Based on community service, civic responsibility, and academic achievement.

name: New York Women in Communications Scholarship
organization: New York Women in Communications Foundation
description: For students pursuing careers in communications, media, and PR. Applicants must reside or attend school in NY, NJ, CT, or PA. Strong preference for underrepresented students.

name: National Association of Black Journalists Scholarship
description: For Black students pursuing journalism, media, and communications careers. NABJ is the largest organization of journalists of color in the world. Membership required.

name: National Black Nurses Association Scholarship
description: For NBNA members currently enrolled in a nursing program with at least one year of study remaining. Must be in good academic standing. Multiple awards available.

name: Tubman Scholar Scholarship Fund
organization: National Foundation for Portugese American Philanthropy
description: Provides scholarships for Black students pursuing LPN, RN-BSN, or baccalaureate nursing degrees. Supports diversity in the nursing profession.

name: BWISE Scholarship for Nursing Students
organization: Black Women in Science and Engineering (BWISE)
description: Empowers African American female students pursuing degrees in STEM and healthcare fields. Recognizes academic excellence and leadership potential.

name: APA Minority Fellowship Program
description: Supports ethnic minority students pursuing doctoral degrees in psychology and neuroscience. Undergraduate juniors planning to pursue graduate education are encouraged to apply.

name: NASW Minority Fellowship Program
organization: National Association of Social Workers Foundation
description: For students pursuing master's degrees in social work with interest in health or mental health practice and commitment to working in African-American communities.

name: Congressional Black Caucus Foundation Scholarship
description: Supports African American students pursuing careers in public service, policy, and civic leadership. Recipients gain access to the CBC's vast network of legislators and policy leaders.

name: Charles B. Rangel International Affairs Scholarship
description: Prepares high-achieving students for careers in the U.S. Foreign Service. Includes graduate school tuition, living stipend, two internships, and mentoring. Highly competitive.

name: Thurgood Marshall College Fund Law Scholarship
description: Supports HBCU students pursuing pre-law and legal studies. Recipients gain access to TMCF's legal network, clerkship connections, and career development resources.

name: National Society of Black Engineers Scholarship
description: Multiple scholarship programs for Black engineering students. NSBE membership required. Includes corporate-sponsored awards from Boeing, Google, Lockheed Martin, and others.

name: STEM Scholarship for Underrepresented Students
description: Supports underrepresented minority students pursuing STEM degrees. Need-based award helping close equity gaps in science and technology fields.

name: ACS Scholars Program
description: Renewable scholarships for underrepresented minority students pursuing chemistry, biochemistry, or chemical engineering. Includes mentorship and research opportunities.

name: Society of Toxicology Undergraduate Diversity Program
description: For underrepresented minority undergraduate students pursuing degrees in toxicology, biology, or chemistry. Includes travel funding to attend the annual SOT meeting.

name: UNCF/Merck Science Initiative Scholarship
description: For African American undergraduate students planning to pursue doctoral degrees in biomedical sciences, chemistry, or engineering. Highly prestigious and competitive.

name: William H. Davis Scholarship for Women in STEM
description: For female students majoring in STEM fields at UNCF member institutions. Supports African American women pursuing careers in science and technology.

name: National Association of Negro Business and Professional Women's Scholarship
description: Supports African American women pursuing business degrees. Encourages leadership development and community service alongside academic excellence.

name: AIGA Worldstudio Scholarship
description: Promotes diversity in design by supporting underrepresented students in visual communication, graphic design, and digital arts. Based on commitment to social change and environmental responsibility.

name: Lift Up Our Voices Scholarship
description: Increases representation of women-identifying students and students of color in music composition. Supports diverse voices in the music industry.

name: Thurgood Marshall Leadership Scholarship
description: Supports full-time students at TMCF-affiliated HBCUs who are active in leadership and community service. For undergraduate and graduate female students.

name: Rodney C. Adkins STEM Scholarship
description: For African American male students majoring in STEM fields. Named after IBM's first Black senior vice president, this scholarship promotes diversity in tech leadership.

name: National Society of High School Scholars Foundation Scholarship
description: Merit-based scholarship for academically outstanding students demonstrating leadership and community involvement. Open to all majors at accredited institutions.

name: United Health Foundation Diverse Scholars Initiative
description: For underrepresented students pursuing health-related degrees. Encourages diversity in healthcare professions and supports students committed to serving diverse communities.

name: Dr. Blanca Moore-Velez Woman of Substance Scholarship
description: Awarded to African American women demonstrating academic excellence, leadership, and community service. Named in honor of Dr. Blanca Moore-Velez.

name: UNCF Tampax Flow It Forward Scholarship
description: For Black and minority women at UNCF member institutions. Supports female students in achieving their academic goals regardless of their field of study.

name: ITE Diversity Scholars Program
description: Supports underrepresented students pursuing careers in transportation engineering and planning. Covers conference attendance and provides mentorship from transportation professionals.

name: American Meteorological Society Minority Scholarship
description: For minority students entering their freshman or sophomore year who plan to pursue a career in the atmospheric, oceanic, or hydrologic sciences.

name: UNCF Olay Face the STEM Gap Scholarship
description: For Black and minority women pursuing STEM degrees at UNCF member institutions. Aimed at closing the gender and racial gap in science fields.

name: Actuarial Diversity Scholarship
description: Promotes diversity in the actuarial profession. For African American, Hispanic, and Native American students pursuing actuarial science or mathematics degrees.

name: National Association of Black Accountants Scholarship
description: Multiple scholarships for Black students pursuing accounting and finance degrees. NABA membership encouraged. One of the top resources for Black accounting students.

name: HBCU Pathways Internship & Scholarship Program
description: 10-week paid internship program for HBCU juniors and seniors passionate about sports, entertainment, and hospitality. Includes mentoring, networking, and real-world industry experience in Atlanta.

name: Thurgood Marshall HBCU All-Majors Scholarship
description: Open to sophomores, juniors, and seniors at TMCF-affiliated HBCUs. Requires financial need and a minimum 3.0 GPA. One of the largest scholarships available to HBCU students.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Educational Advancement Foundation Scholarship
description: For students demonstrating academic excellence, leadership, and community service. AKA membership not required. Open to all majors at accredited institutions.
